summary: Rich soundboard recording of a stellar ’79 show highlighted by a smoking Scarlet>Fire and superb Terrapin Station
review: The Miller‑remastered SBD captures the band firing on all cylinders, with Brent’s organ/Rhodes work and Jerry’s inspired guitar leading an unforgettable performance. Reviewers rave about the extended Scarlet>Fire and a standout Terrapin Station, noting the recording is clear and full‑bodied despite occasional missing track numbers.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give five stars for performance and praise the soundboard quality; only one low rating cites missing track labels, resulting in a strong overall score around 4.3.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Miller’s remaster provides a rich, clear mix; reviewers note decent sounding SBD with no major drop‑outs, though one user complained about missing track numbers in the stream.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Band described as playing as one, perfect from beginning to end; the show flows naturally with extended jams and cohesive energy.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for playing unlike any other ’79 performance.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted as right there with Jerry, holding his own strong bass work.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned alongside Phil as part of the tight ensemble.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s organ and Rhodes piano are described as “ON FIRE” and quintessential ‘79 form.
